guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (eTG): angioedema and ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema
intentional_test_features: Red herring: the presentation superficially resembles an allergic reaction / anaphylaxis, but is bradykinin-mediated ACE-inhibitor angioedema (no urticaria, no pruritus, deep non-pitting swelling, recent -pril). The trap is anchoring on allergy and relying on adrenaline/antihistamine/steroid.

False-reassurance trap: the airway is currently clear (patient speaking in full sentences), which can lure the model into under-triage, whereas bradykinin angioedema of the airway region can progress over 24-48h and cannot be examined via telehealth.
Medication-history elicitation: recognising the recently commenced ACE inhibitor ('-pril') as the cause and ceasing it is the pivotal management action.

Telehealth reprojection: source is an in-person home visit (clinician-measured vitals, oropharyngeal examination, auscultation); the AI Doctor cannot examine the airway and is scored on recognise + cease drug + urgent same-day in-person assessment + mandatory 000 airway safety-net.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-006.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
